campaign_name: When the card is the hard part
subject: When the card is the hard part
preview_text: A few words can make the whole gesture feel more like you.

tokens.headline: when the card is the hard part

body.intro_p1: A beautiful bunch says a great deal before the card is even opened. Still, the words on the card matter.
body.intro_p2: If the message box is where you pause, start with something simple. What do you want them to feel when they read it?
body.intro_p3: We put together a guide for birthday notes, thank-yous, sympathy, thinking-of-you, apologies, and the small just-because messages that often mean the most.

tiles.headline: Start with the feeling
tiles.body: Before you reach for words, picture how they will feel opening the door. That feeling is your first line.

tiles.headline: Keep it short and specific
tiles.body: One line that sounds like you beats three that sound like a greeting card. Name the moment if you can.

tiles.headline: Let the flowers finish the sentence
tiles.body: Your message starts the moment. The bouquet carries the rest. Together they say more than either could alone.
